name: Imperva Custom Rule — API Authentication Brute Force Protection
objective: Apply a defensive Imperva baseline for repeated authentication attempts against API login, token, and session endpoints.
risk_mitigated: Credential stuffing and brute-force traffic against authentication APIs that can lead to account takeover and operational disruption.
potential_side_effects: Shared NAT clients, QA automation, or trusted partner integrations may trigger thresholds if exemptions are not defined carefully.
tuning_notes: Start with alert or monitor mode where available. Apply separate thresholds for public login, MFA verification, and token refresh endpoints to avoid over-blocking trusted traffic.
deployment_notes: Validate threshold logic using Imperva event logs and sampled requests before changing the rule action to a hard block in production.
monitoring_notes: Track top source IPs, targeted authentication paths, blocked user-agents, and burst timing. Correlate with identity telemetry for failed logins.

imperva_rule.notes: Scope by hostname or API service group where possible and prefer a short observation period before enforcement.
